1. the general solution for $ P(t) $ is given by the linear differential equation:

$$
\frac{dP}{dt} = 0.3P \left(1 - \frac{P}{1000}\right) + 0.1 \sin\left(\frac{2\pi t}{12}\right)P
$$

this is a first-order linear differential equation, and the general solution can be found using an integrating factor. the solution will involve both the homogeneous and particular solutions.

2. the maximum population occurs when the derivative $ \frac{dP}{dt} = 0 $, and the sinusoidal term $ \sin\left(\frac{2\pi t}{12}\right) $ reaches its peak. the peak of the sinusoidal term occurs at $ t = 3 $ months, which is the midpoint of the year. the population will reach its maximum at this time, and the impact of the sinusoidal term will cause the population to peak at this point, influencing the enthusiast's beetle-watching activities by suggesting the best time to observe the peak.
